1.title: Solomon invites his sons to seek wisdom. 'The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge.' He warns against rejecting instruction and the snares of the wicked.
1.text: Prologue: Personified Wisdom cries out in the streets, calling the simple to repent. Those who reject her will face ruin.

1.question: What is the beginning of knowledge per 1:7?
To whom does personified Wisdom cry?
How do the wicked's snares work?
What is the reward of heeding wisdom?

2.title: He who seeks wisdom as hidden treasure will find it. Wisdom preserves from the path of evil and the strange woman.
2.text: Wisdom grants understanding, discretion, and guardance. It protects the upright man from the snares of infidelity and spiritual death.

2.question: How is wisdom sought in this chapter?
How is the wise man distinguished?

3.title: Mercy and truth must not be forgotten. Trust in the Lord with all your heart. Wisdom is more precious than gold and jewels.
3.text: The Lord disciplines whom He loves as a father. Wisdom is a tree of life. Blessed is the man who finds understanding.

3.question: How does the Lord show love?
To what is wisdom's price compared?
What is promised to those honoring parents?
What is wisdom to those keeping it?
What did God create with wisdom?
How does this chapter end?
options: Com promessa de honra para sábios

4.title: The father imparts wisdom to his sons. 'Guard your heart above all things.' The path of the just shines like the light of dawn.
4.text: A father's instruction is precious treasure. Avoid the paths of wickedness. Thoughts lead to death or life.

4.question: What should we seek above all?
How is the path of the just described?
options: Como luz cada vez mais clara

4.question: To what is the wicked's path compared?

5.title: Warning against the adulteress woman. Her lips drip honey, but her end is bitterness. 'Guard your heart, for from it flow the springs of life.'
5.text: Adultery brings death, shame, and poverty. The righteous are preserved and delight in their own wife.

5.question: From whom should we guard ourselves?
How is the adulterer's end described?
Of what death does this speak?

6.title: Warnings against reckless pledges for others. Observe the ant: it works tirelessly. Seven things are abominable to the Lord, including a heart that devises evil.
6.text: The ant works without being commanded. The Lord hates proud eyes, a lying tongue, and one who sows discord.

7.title: A story of the foolish young man seduced by the adulteress. Wisdom is guarded as the apple of one's eye. Its rejection brings ruin.
7.text: The adulteress seduces with sweet words. The young man is led like an animal to slaughter. His path leads to death.

8.title: Wisdom cries out to be heard. She is more precious than gold or silver. The Lord possessed her at the beginning, before creating.
8.text: Wisdom was with God, joyful and playful. Whoever finds her finds life and the Lord's favor.

9.title: Wisdom builds a house and prepares a feast. 'The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.' Folly also invites.
9.text: Wisdom invites the simple to learn. Folly tempts the innocent with false promises.

10.title: The proverbs of Solomon begin. A wise son brings joy to his father; a foolish son brings sorrow. Justice delivers from death.
10.text: Contrast between righteous and wicked. Wisdom is on the lips of the discerning. The wise heart chooses apt words.

11.title: Pride goes before destruction; humility before honor. Integrity guides the upright; deceit destroys the unfaithful.
11.text: Righteousness redeems life; wickedness enslaves. The wise cultivate life; the foolish destroy it.

12.title: He who loves discipline loves knowledge. False words wound; truth heals. The lazy man craves; the diligent prosper.
12.text: The mouth of the wise brings healing; the fool's brings ruin. Anxiety weighs the heart; kind words gladden it.

13.title: The wise son heeds instruction; the mocker does not. The lazy man desires but gains nothing; the diligent is satisfied.
13.text: Hope deferred afflicts the heart; desire fulfilled is a tree of life. Wisdom opens paths.

14.title: The wisdom of women builds their house; foolishness destroys it. The fear of the Lord is a fountain of life. Anger stirs strife; patience calms it.
14.text: The quick-tempered commit folly; the discerning are wise. The path of life leads upward for the wise, downward for the fool.

15.title: A soft answer turns away wrath; a harsh word stirs it. The tongue of the wise is a tree of life; the perverse wounds the spirit.
15.text: The merry have a continual feast; the sad a heavy heart. The way of the impatient is harsh; the prudent's is pleasant.

16.title: Man proposes, but God disposes. Righteousness exalts a nation; sin shames it. A calm heart is the life of the flesh; envy rots the bones.
16.text: The wise discern what is just; the fool sows discord. Justice leads to good; wickedness to death.

17.title: Better a dry crust with peace than a house full of strife. The prudent servant will inherit; the wicked will suffer. A joyful heart nourishes the body.
17.text: He who covers a transgression seeks love; he who repeats it separates friends. A rebuke impresses the wise more than a hundred blows to the fool.

18.title: He who separates seeks his own desire and breaks with sound judgment. Death and life are in the power of the tongue; those who love it eat its fruit.
18.text: A man's heart plans his way; but the Lord directs his steps. A good name is more valuable than riches.

19.title: Better the poor who walks in integrity than he with perverse lips. The wise fear and turn from evil; the fool is reckless and confident.
19.text: An angry heart stirs strife; the stingy provoke contention. The lazy man suffers hunger; the diligent prosper.

20.title: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging. It is foolish to be stirred by anger. The wise understand the will of the Lord and fulfill it.
20.text: The Lord abhors false weights; just scales are His delight. A king reigns by righteousness; the wicked by oppression.

21.title: The king's heart is directed by the Lord like a river. Better to have a good name and grace than much gold and silver. The lazy man craves all day.
21.text: He who pursues justice finds life, justice, and honor. A proud person sows strife; a humble one reaps peace.

22.title: A good name is more desirable than great riches. The rich and poor meet together; the Lord made them all. The wise scale the city of the mighty.
22.text: Discipline your daughter and she will give you rest. Train a child in the way he should go; when old, he will not depart from it.

23.title: Hold fast to instruction all your life. Buy the truth and do not sell it; likewise wisdom, instruction, and understanding. A joyful heart does good like medicine.
23.text: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice; he who has a wise son will delight in him. May your father and mother be glad.

24.title: A house is built by wisdom; its rooms are filled with knowledge. The wise man is stronger than the warrior. There is greater joy in planning victory.
24.text: No one succeeds alone; victory comes through many advisors. Rise like the righteous before dawn; observe justice.

25.title: These also are Solomon's proverbs, copied by Hezekiah's men. It is the glory of God to conceal a thing, the glory of a king to search it out.
25.text: Sweetness is to the soul; likewise, knowledge is sweet to the bones. A word spoken at the right time is like apples of gold in settings of silver.

26.title: Like snow in summer, so honor does not fit a fool. The lazy man is wiser in his own eyes than seven who can give good reasons.
26.text: A soft tongue breaks hard bones. He who covers hatred has lying lips; he who spreads slander is a fool.

27.title: Do not boast about tomorrow. Praise is pleasant, but not from your own mouth. Iron sharpens iron; a man sharpens the countenance of his friend.
27.text: He who tends the fig tree will eat its fruit. As water reflects the face, so a man's heart reflects the other's.

28.title: The wicked flee though no one pursues; the righteous are as bold as a lion. The land is troubled by many rulers; it is preserved by wisdom.
28.text: A ruler who oppresses the poor is a destroying rain. He who loves wisdom gladdens his father; he who keeps the law is blessed.

29.title: He who is often rebuked but refuses correction will suddenly be destroyed. When the righteous increase, the people rejoice; when the wicked rule, the people groan.
29.text: Where there is no vision, the people perish. With many advisors comes safety. Justice and mercy together bring peace.

30.title: The words of Agur son of Jakeh. Agur proclaims: I am too foolish to be a man. Every word of God is pure; he is a shield for those who trust in him.
30.text: There are four things on earth that are small but very wise: the ant, the badger, the locusts, and the lizard. Everything has its time and purpose under heaven.

31.title: The words of King Lemuel, which his mother taught him. A king should not give his strength to women. Who can find a virtuous woman? Her value far exceeds rubies.
31.text: The virtuous woman works with eager hands. She extends her hand to the needy; she distributes food to the poor. Her beauty fades, but she fears the Lord.
